description.text:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 39.0.0 to 39.1.0.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v39.1.0

Release Notes for v39.1.0

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue on Wayland (Linux) where right-clicking in the titlebar could break mouse interactions. #48758 (Also in 38, 40)
  • Fixed an issue that menu items on macOS could not be disabled under all circumstances. #48711 (Also in 37, 38)
  • Fixed incorrect signal variable reference in nan-spec-runner that could cause install failures to go undetected. #48709 (Also in 38)

Other Changes

  • Updated Chromium to 142.0.7444.59. #48723
  • Updated Node.js to v22.21.1. #48616
Commits
  • cc78584 fix: release mouse buttons on focus loss on Wayland (#48758)
  • 2e9f754 docs: add release timeline for Electron 40 (#48751)
  • 1d300ad docs: clarify BrowserWindow and App API support status on Wayland (#48746)
  • f404955 refactor: remove redundant map lookups in browser/api/menu.ts (#48731)
  • 0c03766 chore: bump node to v22.21.1 (39-x-y) (#48616)
  • 6938c90 chore: bump chromium to 142.0.7444.59 (39-x-y) (#48723)
  • 32f9752 docs: use relative link for OffscreenSharedTexture (#48719)
  • 429b537 docs: add caveats for Electron PipeWire implementation (#48713)
  • f4dede9 fix: use correct signal variable in nan-spec-runner install check (#48709)
  • eb0f7e6 fix: allow disabling all NSMenuItems (#48711)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

summary: Zettlr on MacOS clearly slower since version 3.4.2

description.text:
### Description

Hi

I use Zettlr on MacOS (Ventura 13.6.6) and I noticed that Zettlr is clearly slower since the 3.4.2 version. 

So I have tested several old versions to see when it begins to go wrong from the 3.3.1. Between each installation, I have uninstalled Zettlr and deleted the /Users/XXX/Zettlr folder.

- From 3.3.1 to 3.4.1, everything works fine.
- With 3.4.2, it begins to slow, even when I launch and quit Zettlr several times.
- With 3.4.6, I can’t work normally because it is too slow (30 s approx just to launch the program, same when I open some little files of the local documentation of Zettlr).

Libreoffice is faster than Zettlr so I think it is not a problem on my computer.




### Reproducing

Just launch and try to use Zettlr.

### Zettlr version

From 3.4.2 to 3.4.6

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Mac OS Ventura 13.6.6

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Adding OPDS support as a bibliography source

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

It would be nice if Zettlr supported using an [OPDS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_Publication_Distribution_System) link (or multiple links) as a bibliography source.

This would be nice because then Zettlr would no longer require a local directory to query for bibliographic information, especially when the libraries get very large. I'd be able to point Zettlr to either a publicly accessible OPDS server (here is a [MobileRead Wiki page](https://wiki.mobileread.com/wiki/OPDS) containing examples in various languages) and/or one that I self-host on my network.

Users are able to self-host OPDS catalogs easily using FOSS software like [Calibre](https://manual.calibre-ebook.com/generated/en/calibre-server.html) or [Jellyfin](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin-plugin-opds). This gives access to both bibliographic information and the files for reading.

### Example Workflow

1. User acquires one or more OPDS links of their choice.
2. Instead of pointing Zettlr to an exported bibliography database as mentioned in the [documentation](https://docs.zettlr.com/en/core/citations/), they paste the one or more links in for Zettlr to source for bibliography information.

### Caveats

This would only add to the available bibliography types that can be used.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Unlink internal links in file manager, search, auto-label, etc.

description.text:
### Description

Currently, in the file manager, searches, auto complete, and the new internal label feature, links and all text are shown entirely un-rendered.

This is what it looks like in my file explorer

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cad3a2d4-222d-4229-bf9c-95957d07631c)

and an example note to explain why there might be a leading link in a note, plus the autocomplete bar.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db61552f-86ff-4d09-8c88-6f4cf902578f)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4afd946-6291-4151-9aa7-640e9dd6eafc)

It really messes up the auto label especially

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f99e2fc-41d1-44b6-8355-29a33f4baeef)

### Proposed Changes

This could be augmented in one of two ways. The first is that the "Titles" should simply be parsed and stripped of internal links, in the same way that "Export/InternalLinks/Unlink" functions. This can either happen right off the bat in the behind the scene title grabbing, or only when these file titles are being displayed. A setting for this would be fine.

As a little side note, stripping them of italics and whatnot as if they were passed through ctrl-shft-v would be nice but a little different.

TLDR: Titles should be stripped of links but leave the link text or label.

### Caveats

This may interfere with someone else's workflow, particularly users who use non UID file names, but as this only removes the brackets and labels it should not affect them at all? 

If there are any complaints, I am glad to have this simply be a setting.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Beta (if applicable)

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Zorin OS

### Additional Information

_No response_
